landmark_structure.justification: Both skip links present at top of document ('Skip to main content' and 'Skip to navigation'). `<aside>` wraps the sidebar, two `<nav>` elements each have distinct `aria-label` values, `<main>` correctly contains only page-specific content, and the `<section>` is labelled via `aria-labelledby`.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single `<h1>` for 'Projects', a visually-hidden `<h2>` labels the project list section, and `<h3>` headings name each project card — a clean h1→h2→h3 hierarchy with no skipped levels.

element_choice.justification: All interactive elements are native: `<button popovertarget>` for the user menu trigger, `<a>` for Profile/Preferences, `<button>` for Sign Out, `<time datetime>` for all dates, `<article>` for project cards. Avatar `alt=""` is correct since the button already contains the visible name 'Alex Chen'.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used only where necessary: `aria-label` on both `<nav>` and `<aside>` landmarks (appropriate given multiple same-type landmarks), `aria-current="page"` on the active nav item, `aria-labelledby` on the `<section>`. No redundant role attributes anywhere. `tabindex="-1"` on `<main>` is correct to support skip-link focus.

list_semantics.justification: Both nav sections use `<ul>` for their items, the popover user menu uses `<ul>`, project cards are wrapped in `<ul>`, and card metadata uses `<dl>` for key-value pairs — all list types correctly chosen for their content.

expectations_met: Sidebar navigation uses <nav> with a descriptive aria-label ('Primary')
<main> wraps only the projects content area; sidebar is in <aside> outside <main>
Both 'Skip to main content' and 'Skip to navigation' skip links are the first focusable elements
Two <nav> elements each have distinct labels ('Primary' and 'Secondary')
Nav items are in <ul> lists within each <nav>
User dropdown uses <button popovertarget> with Popover API (<ul popover>) — not <details>/<summary>
'Profile' and 'Preferences' are <a> links; 'Sign Out' is a <button>
Project cards are in a <ul> list
Status badges convey status via text ('Active', 'Paused', 'Complete'), not colour alone
Dates use <time> with datetime attribute on all three cards
The secondary nav section has aria-label='Secondary' distinguishing it from primary nav
Logo is an link containing an with alt text
expectations_missed: Key expectation states project card names should be <h2>, but the implementation uses <h3> — this is architecturally correct given the intermediate visually-hidden <h2> section heading, but diverges from the stated expectation
additional_observations: The visually-hidden <h2> 'Project list' inside a <section aria-labelledby> is an excellent pattern that both labels the landmark and justifies the h3 card headings
Using <dl> for card metadata (Status, Last updated, Members) is a strong semantic choice for key-value data
<aside aria-label='Sidebar'> correctly names the complementary landmark to disambiguate it
